---
title: "Recycling and Bypass Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to Recycling and Bypass

Main title explanation

## Recycling Before Recycling Was Cool

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/R7tIQyBUWn8?si=kR6xXebWj14tVs-D" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](43-RecycleBypassPartVisuals.qmd)

## Leaving Radiator Springs In the Dust

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/tjzDu7_n-NI?si=RkVMUYxMMiKriMw1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](44-RecycleBypassPart2Visuals.qmd)

## The Symbolic Meaning of Recycling

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/CF18jpqZzgc?si=-tqvFS1uczIsknEf" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](45-RecycleSymbSolVisuals.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [Recycling Examples](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/RecycleExamples.ods) – A spreadsheet with the examples from *Recycling Before Recycling Was Cool* and *Leaving Radiator Springs In the Dust*
- [Symbolic Recycling Example](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/material/SymbolicRecycleExample.ods) – A spreadsheet with the example from *The Symbolic Meaning of Recycling* 

## Definitions

[PFD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Process_flow_diagram) Process Flow Diagram or flowsheet.
:    A schematic drawing of the mixers, dividers, separators and reactors used in the process.

Recycle
:   Part of a product stream is returned to the feed of a process.

Bypass
:   Part of the feed is routed around a process and mixed with the product.

Purge
:   A stream used to prevent buildup of trace components in a recycle loop.

Tie Component
:   A component of the feed which is unchanged by a process. Often useful for calculations but never necessary.

Overall Balance
:   Treating a PFD as a black box with only inputs and outputs.

Mixer

:   Mixes or combines multiple input streams into a single output stream.

Divider

:   Divides the input stream into multiple output streams, each with the same composition as the input stream, but with differing flow rates.

Separator

:   Separates the input stream into multiple output streams, each with a different composition than the input stream. The fraction of a given species in the input stream that exits in a given output stream is the split fraction for that species in that output stream.

Split Fraction

:   The fraction of a given species in the input stream that exits in a given output stream is the *split fraction* for that species in that output stream. For separators with two output streams, the split fractions for a given species in the two output streams sum to 1. For separators with more than two output streams, the split fractions for a given species in all output streams sum to 1.

Composition
:   Composition is the same as the mole or mass fraction of each species in a stream. See [*What the Frac?*](https://youtu.be/qJERzRm7xc0) for more details.


# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**When You Saw It, What Was the Extent of Your Reaction? Part 2**<br/>*Chemical Reactor Multiple Reactions with Example, $\xi$ and $f_{\mathrm{A}}$*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/XmLXR_KOZMk), [Info Page](../../balances/material/FractionalConversionReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/42-UnitOpsReactPart2Visuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**When You Saw It, What Was the Extent of Your Reaction? Part 3**<br/>*Chemical Reactor Yield and Selectivity*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/PPi-ka3iRvI), [Info Page](../../balances/material/FractionalConversionReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/42.5-UnitOpsReactPart3Visuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**How Did You Think I Would React?**<br/>*Basic Kinetic Reactor Mole Balance*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/_qxHszVZYGk), [Info Page](../../balances/material/KineticReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/46-KineticsIntroJTFVisuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**Wishing Upon a CSTR**<br/>*Batch, CSTR, PFR*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/6tnVFq7dSbE), [Info Page](../../balances/material/KineticReactorsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/material/47-BatchCSTRPFRIntroVisuals.qmd)
